snp3.10:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
snp3.10:1.3:
Then the monk Kokālika went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, bowed down to him and sat to one side.
As he was sitting there, he said to the Blessed One,
snp3.10:1.4:
“Lord, Sāriputta & Moggallāna have evil desires.
They have fallen under the sway of evil desires.”
snp3.10:2.1: When this was said, the Blessed One said to him,
snp3.10:2.2:
“Don’t say that, Kokālika.
Don’t say that, Kolālika.
snp3.10:2.3: Make your mind confident in Sāriputta & Moggallāna.
snp3.10:3.2: A third time, the monk Kokālika said to the Blessed One,
snp3.10:3.3:
“Even though the lord Blessed One inspires conviction and faith in me, Sāriputta & Moggallāna have evil desires.
They have fallen under the sway of evil desires.”
snp3.10:3.4: A third time, the Blessed One said to him,
snp3.10:3.5:
“Don’t say that, Kokālika.
Don’t say that, Kolālika.
snp3.10:3.6: Make your mind confident in Sāriputta & Moggallāna.
snp3.10:4.1:
Then the monk Kokālika, getting up from his seat, bowed down to the Blessed One,
circumambulated him, keeping him to his right, and departed.
snp3.10:4.2: Then not long after he had departed, Kokālika’s whole body became covered with boils the size of mustard seeds.
snp3.10:4.3:
From the size of mustard seeds, they became the size of green gram.
From the size of green gram, they became the size of black beans.
From the size of black beans, they became the size of jujube pits.
From the size of jujube pits, they became the size of jujube fruits.
From the size of jujube fruits, they became the size of myrobalans.
From the size of myrobalans, they became the size of unripe vilva fruits.
From the size of unripe vilva fruits, they became the size of small jackfruits.
When they were the size of small jackfruits, they burst.
Blood and pus flowed out.
snp3.10:4.4: Then the monk Kokālika died of that disease and, having died,
snp3.10:4.5: he reappeared in the Paduma hell for engendering hatred in his heart against Sāriputta & Moggallāna.
snp3.10:5.1:
Then Brahmā Sahampati, in the far extreme of the night,
his extreme radiance lighting up the entirety of Jeta’s Grove, went to the Blessed One.
On arrival, having bowed down to him, he stood to one side.
As he was standing there, he said to the Blessed One,
snp3.10:5.2: “Lord, the monk Kokālika has died and, having died,
snp3.10:5.3: has reappeared in the Paduma hell for engendering hatred in his heart against Sāriputta & Moggallāna.”
snp3.10:5.4: That is what Brahmā Sahampati said.
snp3.10:5.5:
Having said that, having bowed down, he circumambulated the Blessed One,
keeping him to his right, and disappeared right there.
snp3.10:6.1: Then when the night had passed, the Blessed One addressed the monks,
snp3.10:6.2:
“Monks, last night Brahmā Sahampati, in the far extreme of the night,
his extreme radiance lighting up the entirety of Jeta’s Grove, came to me.
On arrival, having bowed down to me, he stood to one side.
As he was standing there, he said to me,
‘Lord, the monk Kokālika has died and, having died,
has reappeared in the Paduma hell for engendering hatred in his heart against Sāriputta & Moggallāna.’
snp3.10:6.3:
That is what Brahmā Sahampati said.
Having said that, having bowed down, he circumambulated me, keeping me to his right, and disappeared right there.”
snp3.10:7.1: When this was said, a certain monk said to the Blessed One,
snp3.10:7.2: “Lord, how long is the measure of the lifespan in the Paduma hell?”
snp3.10:7.3: “Long, monk, is the measure of the lifespan in the Paduma hell.
snp3.10:7.4:
It’s not easy to calculate ‘this many years’ or ‘this many hundreds of years’ or
‘this many thousands of years’ or ‘this many hundreds of thousands of years.’”
snp3.10:7.5: “But, lord, can a simile be made?”
snp3.10:7.6: “It can, monk,” said the Blessed One.
snp3.10:8.1:
“Suppose that there were a twenty-measure Kosalan cartload of sesame seeds.
From that, a man would remove a sesame seed every hundred years.
snp3.10:8.2:
That twenty-measure Kosalan cartload of sesame seeds would come to an
end in this manner more quickly than a single Abbuda [Swelling] hell.
snp3.10:8.3: Like twenty Abbuda hells is one Nirabbuda [Free from Swelling] hell.
snp3.10:8.4: Like twenty Nirabbuda hells is one Ababa [Alas!] hell.
snp3.10:8.5: Like twenty Ababa hells is one Ahaha hell.
snp3.10:8.6: Like twenty Ahaha hells is one Aṭaṭa hell.
snp3.10:8.7: Like twenty Aṭaṭa hells is one Kumuda [White Water Lily] hell.
snp3.10:8.8: Like twenty Kumuda hells is one Sogandhika [Fragrant White Water Lily] hell.
snp3.10:8.9: Like twenty Sogandhika hells is one Uppalaka [Blue Lotus] hell.
snp3.10:8.10: Like twenty Uppalaka hells is one Puṇḍarīka [White Lotus] hell.
snp3.10:8.11: Like twenty Puṇḍarīka hells is one Paduma [Red Lotus] hell.
snp3.10:8.12:
“The monk Kokālika has reappeared in the Paduma hell,
for engendering hatred in his heart against Sāriputta & Moggallāna.”
snp3.10:8.13: That is what the Blessed One said.
snp3.10:8.14: Having said that, the One Well-Gone, the Teacher, said further:
snp3.10:9.2: an axe is born in his mouth,
snp3.10:9.4: the fool saying a bad statement.
snp3.10:10.4: and in that wickedness finds no ease.
snp3.10:11.1: Next to nothing is the bad throw
snp3.10:11.2: when one loses money at dice.
snp3.10:11.4: But great is the bad throw
snp3.10:11.5: when one gets angered at those well-gone.
snp3.10:12.4: directing his words & heart toward evil, goes to hell.
snp3.10:13.1: He goes to hell, the one who asserts what didn’t take place,
snp3.10:13.2:
as does the one who, having done, says,
‘I didn’t.’
snp3.10:13.4: after death, in the place beyond.
snp3.10:14.3: The evil comes right back to the fool
snp3.10:14.4: like fine dust thrown against the wind.
snp3.10:15.1: One devoted to the strings of greed
snp3.10:16.1: You with your hard road of a mouth, untrue, ignoble,
snp3.10:16.2: destroyer of progress, evil, doer of wrong,
snp3.10:16.4:
Don’t speak a lot here.
You are headed to hell.
snp3.10:17.1: You scatter dust to your harm.
snp3.10:17.2: You, an offender, malign the good,
snp3.10:17.3: and, having engaged in many sorts of bad conduct
snp3.10:17.4: are going for a long time to the pit.
snp3.10:18.1: For no one’s action is annihilated.
snp3.10:18.2: Surely its owner gets it back.
snp3.10:18.3: An offender, the fool, sees suffering for himself
snp3.10:19.1: He goes to the place set with iron spikes,
snp3.10:19.3: where the food, as is fitting,
snp3.10:19.4: resembles a ball of heated iron.
snp3.10:20.1: When they [the hell-wardens] speak, they don’t speak lovingly.
snp3.10:20.2:
They [the hell beings] can’t run away.
They’re not going to shelter.
snp3.10:20.3: They lie on ashes strewn about.
snp3.10:20.4: They enter a blazing mass of fire.
snp3.10:21.2: they [the hell-wardens] strike them with hammers made of iron.
snp3.10:21.3: Truly, they go to a blind darkness
snp3.10:21.4: that spreads out like a fog.
snp3.10:22.1: Then they enter a copper pot,
snp3.10:22.3: in which they cook for a long, long time,
snp3.10:22.4: bobbing up & down in a mass of fire.
snp3.10:23.2: in a mixture of blood & pus.
snp3.10:23.3: In whatever direction he leans to rest
snp3.10:24.2: in an ooze where worms live,
snp3.10:24.3: and there is no shore to which he can go,
snp3.10:24.4: for the cooking pots all around are all the same.
snp3.10:25.1: Then they enter the sharp sword-leaf forest
snp3.10:25.2: where their limbs are cut off.
snp3.10:25.3: Seizing them by the tongue with a hook,
snp3.10:25.4: they [the hell-wardens] strike them, dragging them back & forth.
snp3.10:26.1: Then they come to the Vettaraṇin, hard to cross,
snp3.10:26.3: and there they fall in, the fools,
snp3.10:27.2: voracious black & spotted dogs, jackals, & flocks of ravens chew on them.
snp3.10:28.1: How hard, indeed, is this way of life there
snp3.10:28.2: that offending people come to see.
snp3.10:28.3: So, for the remainder of life here,
snp3.10:28.4: a person, heedful, should do his duty.
snp3.10:29.3: have been calculated by those who know as five times ten thousand crores,
snp3.10:29.4: plus twelve times one hundred more.
snp3.10:30.1: The length of the hells of suffering described here
snp3.10:30.2: is how long [the hell-beings] will have to dwell there.
snp3.10:30.3: So when in the company of those who are pure, admirable, excellent,
snp3.10:30.4: one should constantly guard one’s words & heart.
